name: Old Time Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a heavy, 2-quart saucepan.
2. Combine sugar, milk, chocolate, salt, and corn syrup in the melted butter.
3. Add marshmallow fluff to the mixture (optional).
4. Heat over medium heat until the sugar dissolves completely.
5. Continue cooking until the mixture reaches the soft-ball stage (234°F/112°C), stirring occasionally if needed.
6. Remove from heat immediately and stir in the butter, allowing it to cool to lukewarm (110°F/43°C) without stirring further. You may let it cool in a bowl of cold water.
7. Add vanilla extract and beat vigorously until the fudge becomes very thick and starts to lose its gloss.
8. Spread the mixture into a buttered shallow pan.
9. Score the fudge while it is still warm, then cut into pieces once it has cooled and firm up.
10. (Optional) Quickly stir in broken nuts at the end of the beating process.
